intro: Nassella mucronata, also known as needle grass or spike fescue, is a perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which may aid in treating wounds, reducing fever, and soothing digestive issues. Nassella mucronata contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its potential therapeutic effects.
